category_title: Academically gifted students who struggle with an excruciatingly slow reading pace
student_description: Dillan is an academically gifted student who struggles with an excruciatingly slow reading pace and decoding difficulties, leading to frustration and avoidance of independent reading. He uniquely requires alternative text access (like audio), short reading chunks paired with immediate verbal processing, and explicit instruction in leveraging his strong background knowledge to comprehend text.

criteria.rationale: Removing the decoding barrier allows Dillan to focus on his strong comprehension skills rather than getting bogged down by his slow reading pace.
criteria.check: The tutor provides or suggests an alternative modality for accessing the text, such as an audiobook, text-to-speech, or reading aloud to the student.

criteria.rationale: Breaking the text into small chunks with immediate verbal processing prevents cognitive overload and helps Dillan clarify confusion before it compounds.
criteria.check: The tutor breaks the reading task into short segments (e.g., 10 minutes) and pairs each segment with an immediate opportunity for verbal discussion or processing.
